subject: Chemistry
solution:
$4 \mathrm{~g}$ of hydrogen $=4$ mole of hydrogen $=4 \times 6.023 \times 10^{23}$ atoms
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 71.0 \mathrm{gm} \text { of chlorine }=\frac{71.0}{71.0}=1 \text { moles of } \\
& \text { chlorine } \\
& 127 \mathrm{gm} \text { of iodine }=\frac{1}{2} \text { mole of }_2 \\
& =\frac{127}{254}=6.023 \times 10^{23} \text { atoms } \\
& 48.0 \mathrm{gm} \text { of magnesium } \\
& =\frac{48.0}{24.0}=2 \times 6.023 \times 10^{23} \times \frac{1}{2} \text { atoms } \\
& \therefore 4.0 \mathrm{gm} \mathrm{H}_2 \text { has largest number of atoms. }
\end{aligned}
$$

In multiplication and division, the result must be reported with no more significant figures as in the measurement with few significant figures

0.02858×0.1120.5702=0.000561

(Or) Reported answer should not be more precise than the least precise term in calculations, so there should be three significant figures in reported answer.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Ratio of mass percentage of C : H = $12 : 1$

Ratio of moles of C : H = $\dfrac{12}{12} : \dfrac{1}{1} = 1 : 1$

The empirical formula is $CH$. Since the hydrocarbon has two carbon atoms, its molecular formula is $C_2H_2$.

Molar mass of $C_2H_2 = 2 \times 12 + 2 \times 1 = 26$ g mol$^{-1}$

Moles of $C_2H_2$ in $3.38$ g = $\dfrac{3.38}{26} = 0.13$ mol

The combustion reaction is:

$C_2H_2 + \dfrac{5}{2}O_2 \rightarrow 2CO_2 + H_2O$

From the stoichiometry, $1$ mole of $C_2H_2$ produces $2$ moles of $CO_2$.

Moles of $CO_2$ produced = $2 \times 0.13 = 0.26$ mol

Molar mass of $CO_2 = 12 + 2 \times 16 = 44$ g mol$^{-1}$

Weight of $CO_2$ formed = $0.26 \times 44 = 11.44$ g

Answer: $11.44$

solution:
$\mathrm{Mg}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{MgCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2$
Volume $\mathrm{H}_2$ evolved $=220 \mathrm{ml}$
Mole of $\mathrm{H}_2=\frac{220 \times 10^{-3}}{22.4}=$ mole of Mg used
$\begin{aligned}
\therefore \text { Mass of Mg used } & =\frac{220 \times 10^{-3}}{22.4} \times 24 \\
& =235.7 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{gm} \\
& =235.7 \mathrm{mg}
\end{aligned}$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
CaCO3s→ΔCaOs+CO2g

MgCO3s→ΔMgOs+CO2g

Let the weight of CaCO3 be x gm

∴ weight of MgCO3=2.21−x gm

Moles of CaCO3 decomposed = moles of CaO formed

x100= moles of CaO formed

∴ weight of CaO formed =x100×56
Moles of MgCO3 decomposed = moles of MgO formed

2.21−x84= moles of MgO formed

∴  weight of MgO formed =2.21−x84×40 ⇒2.21−x84×40+x100×56=1.152

∴x=1.187g= weight of CaCO3 and weight of MgCO3=1.023g

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For (A):

Formula of cyclohexane is $C_6H_{12}$.

Number of atoms per molecule = $6 + 12 = 18$.

Total number of atoms in $2$ moles = $2 \times 18 \times N_A = 36 N_A$.

For (B):

Formula of sucrose is $C_{12}H_{22}O_{11}$.

Molar mass of sucrose = $342$ g/mol.

Number of moles = $\dfrac{684}{342} = 2$ moles.

Number of atoms per molecule = $12 + 22 + 11 = 45$.

Total number of atoms = $2 \times 45 \times N_A = 90 N_A$.

For (C):

Formula of dihydrogen is $H_2$.

Number of moles at STP = $\dfrac{90.8}{22.7} = 4$ moles.

Number of atoms per molecule = $2$.

Total number of atoms = $4 \times 2 \times N_A = 8 N_A$.

Comparing the total number of atoms:

$90 N_A > 36 N_A > 8 N_A$

Therefore, the correct order is $B > A > C$.

Answer: $B>A>C$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Given that, 1 mole of PbS is oxidised by X moles of O3 to get Y moles of O2.

The balanced chemical equation can be written as,

PbS+4O3→PbSO4+4O2

Here x = 4, y = 4 

Hence,

 X+Y=4+4=8
